skill.name: auth-integration
skill.description: Guides implementation of authentication in Next.js apps using Better Auth or NextAuth patterns, including forms, sessions, protected routes, and role-based access control.
skill.summary: Implement secure Next.js authentication with forms, sessions, route guards, and role-based access control.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows the skill is a Markdown guidance file with TypeScript, TSX, and bash examples. The shell execution, browser credential, weak crypto, and heuristic critical findings are false positives caused by code fences, auth terminology, and security checklist text. Low residual risk remains because the skill documents environment variables, .env.local usage, localhost URLs, and external reference links for authentication setup.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backtick-delimited Markdown code blocks as Ruby or shell execution. Reviewed locations are documentation examples, not executable skill code, and no command injection path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is inside Markdown fences containing TypeScript, TSX, or bash examples. No executable script, dynamic command construction, or runtime hook was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Examples for Authentication Secrets
low_findings.description: The skill shows process.env usage and an .env.local template for OAuth and database configuration. This is expected auth documentation, but generated implementations must avoid exposing secrets to client code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable references are clear and intentional. They use placeholder values and instruct secret use through environment variables rather than hardcoding credentials.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The detected URLs are a localhost auth URL and public documentation references. No network request, webhook, telemetry endpoint, or data exfiltration behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in an environment example and references section. There is no code that sends local data or credentials to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access Flag Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The .env.local mention is an example environment file name, and regex character classes were also flagged as hidden file access. No file read or write operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regex lines are password strength checks, and .env.local is shown as a heading inside a bash snippet. There is no filesystem API or command that accesses hidden files.

low_findings.title: Browser Credential Finding Is a Secure Cookie Checklist Item
low_findings.description: The browser credential finding points to a checklist item requiring secure cookies. It does not reference browser credential storage paths or attempt credential collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a security requirement for httpOnly, secure, sameSite cookies. No browser profile files, credential databases, or scraping behavior appear at this location.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Are Auth Terminology Matches
low_findings.description: Static weak-cryptography flags occur on authentication-related prose and authorization examples. The reviewed content recommends bcrypt or argon2 and does not endorse MD5, SHA1, or reversible password storage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant reviewed lines discuss authentication, role checks, or password hashing best practices. The only named password hashing algorithms are bcrypt and argon2, both acceptable choices.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Flags Are Framework Guidance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance flags point to normal framework, role, or database setup guidance. No evidence found of host inspection, identity enumeration, or system discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain validation rules, UI text, and placeholder environment variable names. They do not instruct the agent to enumerate the host or collect system metadata.

content.user_title: Implement Secure Auth for Next.js Apps
content.value_statement: Authentication work often fails when forms, sessions, and authorization are designed separately. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a consistent plan for secure Next.js auth flows.
actual_capabilities: Plans Better Auth or NextAuth setup for Next.js App Router projects.
Generates login, signup, password reset, and verification form guidance.
Defines session management patterns using Zustand and secure cookies.
Designs protected route middleware and client-side auth guards.
Models role and permission checks for admin, teacher, student, and parent users.
Adds security checklists for CSRF, XSS, rate limiting, and audit logging.
limitations: It provides implementation guidance, not a finished authentication library.
Project-specific provider settings, secrets, and database schemas must be supplied by the user.
Generated authorization rules still need server-side review and security testing.
The examples focus on Next.js, Better Auth, NextAuth, Prisma, and Zustand patterns.

use_cases.title: Build a New Auth Flow
use_cases.description: Create a login, signup, email verification, and password reset plan for a Next.js application.

use_cases.title: Add Role-Based Access
use_cases.description: Define roles, permissions, route guards, and server checks for dashboards with different user types.

use_cases.title: Review Auth Security Requirements
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to verify cookies, sessions, rate limits, CSRF protection, and audit logging.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Login
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single login page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this auth-integration skill to create a secure login page for my Next.js App Router project. Include validation, loading states, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Better Auth
prompt_templates.scenario: Provider setup with email and Google auth.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to configure Better Auth with Prisma, email and password login, Google OAuth, email verification, and secure session settings.

prompt_templates.title: Protect Dashboard Routes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add protected routes for /dashboard. Include middleware checks, client auth guards, redirects, and secure loading states.

prompt_templates.title: Design Enterprise RBAC
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced permissions for multiple user groups.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design role-based access control for admin, teacher, student, and parent users. Include permissions, server checks, and audit logging.

output_examples.input: Create authentication for a new Next.js dashboard.
output_examples.output: A structured implementation plan covering auth provider setup, login and signup forms, session hydration, protected middleware, and security checks.

output_examples.input: Add admin-only access to dashboard pages.
output_examples.output: A role and permission model with route guard guidance, unauthorized redirects, server-side verification points, and audit logging recommendations.

output_examples.input: Review my authentication flow before launch.
output_examples.output: A checklist-based review covering secure cookies, CSRF protection, rate limiting, email verification, session expiration, and password handling.

best_practices: Keep secrets in environment variables and never expose them to client components.
Enforce authorization on the server, even when client guards improve user experience.
Test signup, login, logout, session refresh, expired sessions, and unauthorized access paths.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on client-side route guards for sensitive data access.
Do not hardcode OAuth secrets, auth secrets, or database URLs in source files.
Do not ship auth flows without rate limiting, secure cookies, and audit logging.

faq.question: Does this skill implement authentication automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code while they generate project-specific auth code.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Next.js App Router, Better Auth, NextAuth v5 patterns, Prisma, Zustand, React Hook Form, and Zod.

faq.question: Can it support Google OAuth?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance includes Google provider configuration using environment variables for client IDs and secrets.

faq.question: Does it cover role-based access control?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes roles, permissions, middleware checks, component guards, and server-side verification guidance.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples and low-risk auth references, not executable malicious behavior.

faq.question: What should users review before production?
faq.answer: Users should review secret handling, server authorization, cookie settings, rate limits, audit logs, and provider configuration.
